title: Leg of Lamb with Roasted Garlic
subtitle: Herb-studded boneless leg seared hot then slow-roasted to rare, served with whole heads of sweet, spreadable roasted garlic.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Simple pantry herbs let the quality of the lamb and sweet roasted garlic shine.
items: 1 boneless leg of lamb, 4–4½ lb (2–2.25 kg)
8 large heads garlic, plus 2 large cloves
Olive oil for coating
2 tsp finely chopped fresh rosemary or 1 tsp dried
1 tsp finely chopped fresh thyme or ½ tsp dried
ingredients.callout: Roasted garlic tip: Roasting tames garlic's pungency and brings out its sweetness. Simply squeeze each clove so the soft pulp slips out — serve alongside for diners to spread on the lamb.

body.html: A classic Sunday roast: boneless leg of lamb studded with garlic slivers, rubbed with rosemary and thyme, and roasted to a blushing rare. Eight whole garlic heads go in the oven alongside — wrapped in foil, they turn sweet and spreadable for each diner to slather on every slice.

items: Prep the lamb — flatten, stud with garlic slivers, coat with herbs and oil
Sear at 425°F for 15 minutes to build a crust
Reduce to 350°F and roast ~1½ hrs to 125°F internal
Start garlic heads 40–50 min before the lamb is due out
Rest, carve, and serve with pan juices and roasted garlic

body.html: Plan ahead: Garlic needs 40–50 minutes — start it at the right time so it finishes with the lamb.

slides.title: Prep & Season the Lamb
slides.checkboxLabel: Stud with garlic, coat with oil and herbs

items: Place an oven rack in the lower third of the oven and preheat to 425°F (220°C).
Trim any loose fat from the lamb. Open it flat, cut side down, and tuck thin pieces under to create a roast of uniform thickness.
Place the lamb in an oiled heavy roasting pan just large enough to hold it comfortably.
Peel the 2 garlic cloves and cut into 10 slivers. Make 10 small cuts in the lamb and insert a garlic sliver in each.
Coat the lamb with olive oil and sprinkle evenly with rosemary and thyme.

body.html: Uniform thickness matters: tucking thin flaps under ensures even cooking and prevents overcooked edges.

items: Roast at 425°F for 15 minutes to develop a crust.
Reduce heat to 350°F (180°C) and continue roasting until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part reads 125°F (52°C) for rare — about 1½ hours total.

body.html: Doneness guide: 125°F = rare. For medium-rare pull at 130–135°F. Carry-over cooking adds ~5°F during rest.

slides.title: Roast the Garlic Heads
slides.checkboxLabel: Foil-wrap 8 heads, roast until very soft

body.html: Start this step 40–50 minutes before the lamb is due to finish.

items: Remove the outermost papery skin layer from each garlic head.
Cut off the top of each head to expose the cloves.
Coat each head generously with olive oil and wrap individually in foil.
Arrange cut-side up in a baking dish and place in the oven.
Roast until very soft when pressed, about 40–50 minutes.

body.html: Roasting magic: High heat converts harsh raw garlic into sweet, jammy, spreadable cloves. Press from the base to pop them out of their skins.

slides.title: Rest the Lamb
slides.checkboxLabel: Tent with foil and rest 5–10 minutes

body.html: Transfer the lamb to a carving board, cover loosely with aluminum foil, and let stand for 5–10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute for a juicier slice.

slides.title: Carve & Serve
slides.checkboxLabel: Slice across grain, spoon juices, serve with garlic

items: Slice the lamb across the grain and arrange on a warmed platter.
Skim surface fat from the pan juices and spoon the juices over the sliced lamb.
Unwrap the garlic heads and discard the foil.
Serve the roasted garlic alongside — each diner squeezes cloves directly onto their lamb slices.

body.html: Bonus: Serve extra roasted garlic with country bread or toast as an appetizer while the lamb rests.
